findings.title: SQL Injection Pattern: string concat SQL
findings.description: Potential SQL injection via string concat SQL in 'tests\core\test_autofix.py' at line 123.
findings.evidence: tests\core\test_autofix.py:123: before='stmt.execute("SELECT * FROM t WHERE id=" + id)',
findings.recommendation: Use parameterized queries or an ORM. Never construct SQL with user-controlled input.

findings.evidence: tests\plugins\whitebox\test_java_sql_pattern.py:21: "        stmt.execute(\"SELECT * FROM users WHERE id = \" + id);\n"
findings.recommendation: Use parameterized queries or an ORM. Never construct SQL with user-controlled input.

findings.title: Potential SSRF: HTTP Client Called with Variable URL
findings.description: An HTTP client function is called with a non-literal URL argument in 'tests\plugins\whitebox\test_py_ssrf_pattern.py' at line 15. If the URL is user-controlled, this is an SSRF vulnerability.
findings.evidence: tests\plugins\whitebox\test_py_ssrf_pattern.py:15: """requests.get(user_url) with variable argument is flagged as CRITICAL."""
findings.recommendation: Validate and allowlist URLs before making outbound HTTP requests. Never pass raw user input to HTTP client functions.

findings.title: Potential SSRF: HTTP Client Called with Variable URL
findings.description: An HTTP client function is called with a non-literal URL argument in 'vibee_hacker\core\autofix.py' at line 71. If the URL is user-controlled, this is an SSRF vulnerability.
findings.recommendation: Validate and allowlist URLs before making outbound HTTP requests. Never pass raw user input to HTTP client functions.

findings.title: Potential SSRF: HTTP Client Called with Variable URL
findings.description: An HTTP client function is called with a non-literal URL argument in 'vibee_hacker\plugins\whitebox\py_ssrf_pattern.py' at line 13. If the URL is user-controlled, this is an SSRF vulnerability.
findings.evidence: vibee_hacker\plugins\whitebox\py_ssrf_pattern.py:13: # i.e., it's a variable. Pattern: requests.get(something) where 'something' doesn't start with " or '
findings.recommendation: Validate and allowlist URLs before making outbound HTTP requests. Never pass raw user input to HTTP client functions.

findings.title: NoSQL Injection Pattern: $gt operator in query
findings.description: Potential NoSQL injection pattern '$gt operator in query' detected in 'vibee_hacker\plugins\blackbox\nosql_injection.py' at line 17. MongoDB operators in user-controlled input can bypass authentication.
findings.recommendation: Validate and sanitize all user inputs before using them in NoSQL queries. Use schema validation (e.g., Mongoose) and reject input containing MongoDB operators.

findings.title: NoSQL Injection Pattern: collection.find(userInput)
findings.description: Potential NoSQL injection pattern 'collection.find(userInput)' detected in 'vibee_hacker\plugins\whitebox\wb_nosql_injection.py' at line 18. MongoDB operators in user-controlled input can bypass authentication.
findings.recommendation: Validate and sanitize all user inputs before using them in NoSQL queries. Use schema validation (e.g., Mongoose) and reject input containing MongoDB operators.

findings.recommendation: Fix 'CORS all origins wildcard': disable debug mode in production, restrict allowed hosts, and use strong, randomly-generated secret keys.

findings.title: Insecure Cryptography: MD5 used for hashing
findings.description: MD5 is cryptographically broken and should not be used for password hashing or integrity checks. Found in 'tests\plugins\whitebox\test_insecure_crypto.py' at line 18.
findings.evidence: tests\plugins\whitebox\test_insecure_crypto.py:18: """hashlib.md5() in source code is flagged as HIGH."""
findings.recommendation: Replace with secure alternatives: bcrypt/argon2 for passwords, secrets module for tokens, AES-GCM for encryption.

findings.title: No USER directive found — container runs as root by default
findings.description: Without a USER directive Docker defaults to root which increases attack surface. In 'vibee_hacker\plugins\whitebox\dockerfile_check.py'.
findings.recommendation: Add `USER nonroot` or a dedicated application user before the CMD/ENTRYPOINT.

findings.title: Log Injection Risk: User input in Python logger (f-string)
findings.description: User-controlled data appears to be logged without sanitization in 'tests\plugins\whitebox\test_log_injection.py' at line 22. Attackers can inject fake log entries or exploit log parsers.
findings.recommendation: Sanitize or encode user input before logging. Replace newlines (\n, \r) and other control characters. Use structured logging with separate fields for user data.

findings.title: TOCTOU Race Condition: os.path.exists() + open()
findings.description: TOCTOU race condition detected in 'tests\plugins\whitebox\test_wb_race_condition.py' at line 15. Checking file existence then opening it is a race condition.
findings.recommendation: Open the file directly and handle FileNotFoundError/IOError instead of checking existence first. This eliminates the TOCTOU window.
